source_name: Code4rena Dyad Findings and Analysis Report (2024-04)
summary: When a liquidator liquidates a user, he will pay his debt and must receive the debt + 20% bonus in form of collateral (from the user). But now the 20% bonus is based on the user’s (collateral - debt), which removes the entire incentive for liquidation.
root_cause: incorrect accounting or validation logic in Liquidation bonus logic is wrong, causing the protocol to compute or enforce the wrong state transition
broken_invariants: the collateral management path must not allow the condition described by "Liquidation bonus logic is wrong" to change user balances, collateralization, or protocol availability unexpectedly
oracle, TVL, and collateral valuation must use correctly scaled, fresh, and manipulation-resistant inputs
state changes must remain internally consistent across all affected accounting buckets and external integrations
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: When a liquidator liquidates a user, he will pay his debt and must receive the debt + 20% bonus in form of collateral (from the user). But now the 20% bonus is based on the user’s (collateral - debt), which removes the entire incentive for liquidation.

why_not_higher: final C4 severity is Medium; exploitability, affected value, or preconditions are bounded relative to High
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: The bonus should be based on the burned user debt and then must send the liquidator the percentage of liquidated user collateral equal to the burned debt + 20% bonus. This is an example implementation, which gives the desired 20% bonus from the right amount, but need to be tested for further issues. function liquidate( uint id, uint to ) external isValidDNft(id) isValidDNft(to) { uint cr = collatRatio(id); + uint userCollateral = getTotalUsdValue(id); if (cr >= MIN_COLLATERIZATION_RATIO) revert CrTooHigh(); dyad.burn(id, msg.sender, dyad.mintedDyad(address(this), id)); - uint cappedCr = cr < 1e18 ? 1e18 : cr; + uint liquidationEquityShare = 0; + uint liquidationAssetShare = 1e18; + if (cr >= 1.2e18) { + liquidationEquityShare = (dyad.mintedDyad(address(this), id)).mulWadDown(LIQUIDATION_REWARD); + liquidationAssetShare = (dyad.mintedDyad(address(this), id) + liquidationEquityShare).divWadDown(userCollateral); + } - uint liquidationEquityShare = (cappedCr - 1e18).mulWadDown(LIQUIDATION_REWARD); - uint liquidationAssetShare = (liquidationEquityShare + 1e18).divWadDown(cappedCr); uint numberOfVaults = vaults[id].length(); for (uint i = 0; i < numberOfVaults; i++) { Vault vault = Vault
